originalTitle: Malawians repatriated from South Africa amid xenophobia concerns
title: Malawians repatriated from South Africa amid xenophobia concerns
content: Malawi is among several African nations transporting their citizens out of the country following reports of violence against migrants.

originalTitle: 'A World Cup for them not us': Fans' anger at US travel bans and visa restrictions
title: Uh-Oh! Why Some Friends Might Miss the Big Soccer Party!
content:
Hello, Soccer Superstars!

Do you love playing soccer? Or maybe you love watching your favourite teams run and score goals? Soccer is a super fun game! It brings smiles to so many faces all over Africa and the world. Guess what? There is a very, very big soccer party called the World Cup! It only happens once every four years. It's when the best teams from all over the world come together to play. We get super excited when teams from Africa play in the World Cup! We cheer them on very loudly. It's a special time for everyone who loves soccer.

What is the World Cup Anyway?

The World Cup is like the biggest soccer tournament you can imagine. Teams from many, many countries try their best to qualify. Only the best teams get to go. Imagine all the coolest soccer players from different lands playing together. That's the World Cup! They play to see who is the champion of the whole world. Did you know? Billions of people around the world watch the World Cup on TV! It's one of the most-watched events ever.

Where is the Next Big Party?

The next big World Cup party will be held in a country far, far away from Africa. It's in America, which people also call the USA. It will be a really exciting place for soccer! Lots of big stadiums will open their doors. Fans will travel from all over the world. They will come to cheer for their favourite teams and players. Imagine the noise, the colours, and the fun! Everyone dreams of being there to see their heroes play on the big stage.

A Little Problem: Tricky Travel Rules

Now, here is a bit of sad news. Some soccer fans are feeling very sad. They are upset because they might not be able to go to the World Cup in America. Why? Because of something called 'travel rules' and 'special papers'. Countries have rules about who can visit them. These rules can sometimes make it very hard for people to travel. It's like wanting to go to a friend's birthday party, but there are some special rules that make it tricky to get there.

What is a "Visa"? (It's not a type of fruit!)

One of these special papers is called a 'visa'. A visa is like a special permission stamp or sticker. You need it in your passport to visit some countries, like America. It's like getting a special ticket that says, "Okay, you can come into our country!" You have to apply for it and show why you want to visit. Did you know? Not all countries need visas from everyone. But for many people from Africa, a visa is a must-have paper to visit places like the USA.

Why Do Countries Have These Rules?

Countries have these rules for many reasons. Mostly, it's to help keep everyone safe. They want to know who is coming and going into their country. It helps them manage things better. But sometimes, these rules can be very difficult to follow. They can be expensive, or take a very long time to get. This makes it tough for regular fans who just want to go and watch soccer. They just want to support their team, not cause any trouble.

Feeling Left Out of the Fun

This is why fans are feeling left out. They feel like the World Cup is for some people, but not for them. They save their money for years, dreaming of this big trip. Imagine wanting to see your favourite player score a goal live. But you can't go because of these tricky rules. It's very disappointing and makes people feel sad. Did you know? Many fans in Africa are super passionate about soccer! They sing, dance, and celebrate their teams with all their hearts. Missing the World Cup is a big deal for them.

Why It's Important to Be There

It's not just about watching the game on TV. Being at the World Cup in person is a special feeling. You can cheer very loudly with thousands of other fans. You can feel the ground shake when your team scores! You can meet people from all over the world who love soccer just as much as you do. It's like being part of one big, happy soccer family. It's about feeling connected to the game. It's about showing your support directly to your heroes. It's an experience you remember for your whole life.

Dreams of the Green Pitch

For many kids and grown-ups, seeing their favourite team play in the World Cup is a huge dream. For some, it might be the only chance they ever get in their lifetime. These travel rules and visa restrictions make those dreams very, very hard to come true. It's sad when a dream like this feels out of reach. Soccer is a game that brings the world together. It's wonderful when everyone feels welcome to be a part of it, whether they are playing or watching.

Did You Know? More Fun Soccer Facts!

Did you know the very first World Cup was held in 1930? That was a very, very long time ago! Also, Africa has sent many amazing teams to the World Cup over the years. We have seen some incredible players and exciting games from our continent. And guess what? The World Cup has even been held in Africa! South Africa hosted the World Cup in 2010. It was a wonderful celebration for the whole continent!

Hoping for a Happier Future

We hope that maybe, just maybe, the rules can be made a little bit easier. So that more people can share the joy of the World Cup. Everyone should have a chance to cheer! Because soccer is for everyone! It's a game that brings joy and excitement to every corner of the world. Let's hope all fans can one day join the biggest soccer party!
summary: Some soccer fans from Africa and other places are sad because strict travel rules and special papers called visas might stop them from going to the big World Cup party in America.
whyMatters: It matters because the World Cup is a huge soccer party for everyone, and it's sad when fans who love the game cannot go and cheer for their teams because of difficult travel rules.

title: Hundreds of captives freed from Boko Haram mountain hideout
content: The group includes many women and children who were abducted in March from an area close to Cameroon.

title: Why was Israel spared scrutiny for the 1967 USS Liberty attack?
content: US Representative Massie set to honour the crew of the vessel on the House floor, bringing attention to the 1967 attack.

originalTitle: Meta to take legal action against Israeli spyware company NSO
title: WhatsApp is Like a Superhero Stopping Naughty Spies!
content:
Jambo, my amazing friends across Africa! I'm your friendly news reporter, here to tell you a super important story about your phones and keeping your secrets safe. Imagine your phone is like your very own treasure chest, full of cool pictures, fun messages with friends, and maybe even secret game scores!

Your Phone, Your Secret Chest!

Every day, lots of us use phones and tablets. They help us learn, play games, and talk to family far away. But guess what? Your phone also holds many special things, like your photos and the messages you send. These are your private things, just like your favorite toy or your secret diary that only you are allowed to read. Did you know? There are more mobile phones than people in some parts of the world! That's a lot of little treasure chests!

What is WhatsApp?

Many of your grown-ups, and maybe even you, use an app called WhatsApp. It's a fantastic app that lets you send messages, pictures, and even videos to your friends and family all over the world, super fast! It's like sending a magical letter that arrives in seconds. WhatsApp tries very hard to keep your messages and pictures private. They lock them up with a special secret code so that only you and the person you send them to can see them. It's like having a super strong lock on your treasure chest. Did you know? Billions of people use WhatsApp! That's like almost everyone on Earth having a magical message sender!

Naughty Spies and Their Secret Tools

Now, sometimes, there are people who are not so nice. These naughty people want to peek into other people's phones without asking. Why? Maybe they want to find out secrets, or maybe they just want to cause trouble. It's like someone trying to look into your secret diary without your permission. That's not fair, is it? There's a company called NSO. This company makes special secret tools. These tools are like invisible keys or tiny magnifying glasses that can help naughty people try to sneak into phones and look at private messages, even the ones locked up by WhatsApp. We call these tools 'spy tools' because they help people spy on others. Did you know? Your phone has special security features, like a secret password or fingerprint scan, to help keep it safe from prying eyes!

WhatsApp to the Rescue!

Well, guess what? WhatsApp found out about these naughty spy tools that NSO was making. WhatsApp saw that some naughty people were trying to use these tools to trick people into giving away their secrets. This trick is called 'phishing.' Imagine someone pretends to be your best friend, but they're not! They want to trick you into telling them your secret hiding spot. That's a bit like what these bad guys try to do online. But WhatsApp is super smart! They noticed these tricks. They worked really, really hard to stop these naughty spying attempts. It was like a superhero jumping in front of a big problem and saying, "Not on my watch!" They disrupted these attempts, which means they stopped them from working. Hooray for WhatsApp! Did you know? Always ask a grown-up if you see a message or link that looks strange or asks for your private information. Be a smart online detective!

Meta Takes a Stand

WhatsApp is part of a very big company called Meta. Meta is like a giant family that owns many different apps, and WhatsApp is one of its beloved children. When Meta found out that NSO was making these spy tools and that people were trying to use them to snoop on WhatsApp users, they got very serious. Meta decided to take a very big step. They decided to go to 'court.' Going to court is like when grown-ups have a very, very big argument that they can't solve on their own, so they ask a special judge to help decide what is fair and what is not. Meta is going to court because they want to tell NSO, "What you are doing is not okay! It's wrong to make tools that help people snoop on others' private phones!" Did you know? The government of a country called the US has even said that NSO is not trusted because of these security worries. It's like NSO is on a big naughty list for trying to make spy tools.

Why is This Important for YOU?

This whole story is important because it shows us that there are companies, like WhatsApp and Meta, that work hard to keep your private things on your phone safe. They want you to be able to talk to your friends and family without worrying that someone else is secretly listening or looking. It also reminds us to be smart! Always remember to ask a grown-up if something on your phone or tablet looks strange. Don't click on things you don't know, and never share your secret passwords with anyone. Your phone is your treasure chest, and you should always keep it safe! Did you know? Strong passwords are like super secret codes for your phone! They should have a mix of big letters, small letters, numbers, and even special signs to make them extra strong! So, my clever friends, remember this story. Be smart with your phones, and know that there are good people working hard to keep your digital treasure chests safe. Until next time, stay curious and stay safe online!
summary: WhatsApp's owner, Meta, is taking a company called NSO to court for making spy tools that tried to peek into people's private messages on WhatsApp, reminding us that it's important to keep our phones safe.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows big companies are fighting to protect our private messages and pictures on our phones from naughty people who want to snoop, helping us stay safe when we use apps like WhatsApp.

originalTitle: How extreme weather and heat could affect players at World Cup 2026
title: How extreme weather and heat could affect players at World Cup 2026
content: Climate experts warn that 25% of World Cup games could be played in very hot conditions, affecting fans and players.

originalTitle: Iran releases footage of missiles launched at Israel
title: News from Far Away: Sending Kindness, Not Loud Noises!
content:
Hello, amazing kids of Africa! It's your friendly reporter here, bringing you some news from very, very far away. Sometimes, things happen in the world that can sound a little confusing, but we're here to explain it in simple words, just for you! Today, we are talking about two countries that are a long, long way from our beautiful continent of Africa. These countries are called Iran and Israel. Imagine going on a very, very long airplane ride to get there!

What Happened Far Away?

Not long ago, some people in the country called Iran sent some flying objects towards the country called Israel. These flying objects are like very big, very fast rockets. They fly high up in the sky. It was an unfriendly action. The people in Iran were feeling very, very unhappy with the people in Israel. They even said they might send more of these flying objects for a whole week. But grown-ups in many places are working hard to make sure that doesn't happen. Did you know? Our planet Earth is very, very big! There are so many different countries and people all over the world, even if we can't see them from our window!

Why Did This Happen?

Sometimes, countries, just like people, can have big disagreements. Imagine when you and your friend might argue over a toy or a game. It's like that, but much, much bigger. These disagreements can make people very unhappy with each other. When people are unhappy, they sometimes do things that are not very kind. The people in Iran felt they had a big problem with Israel. This made them send those flying objects. It's always much better when people talk about their problems, just like you learn to do with your friends or family. Talking helps us find a way to be kind again. Did you know? Even grown-ups sometimes forget to talk about their problems nicely. That's why we need kind hearts everywhere!

What Are These Flying Objects?

These special flying objects are often called 'missiles' or 'rockets'. They are built to fly very, very fast through the air. They are made to travel a long distance from one place to another very quickly. When they land, they can make loud noises. Think of a fireworks rocket that shoots up into the sky and explodes, but much bigger and not for celebrations. They are not toys. Luckily, Israel has a special way to protect its skies. It's like having a big, invisible shield that can stop many of these flying objects before they land. Did you know? Some rockets are used to send astronauts to space! Those rockets are made for exploration and discovery, which is a very happy use for them!

Was Anyone Hurt?

This is a very important question. Thankfully, because of the special shield in Israel, most of the flying objects sent by Iran were stopped in the sky. This means that many people were kept safe from harm. It's a bit like someone catching a ball before it hits you. Some loud noises were heard, and a few buildings might have been touched, but the important thing is that many, many lives were protected. We are always happy to hear that people are safe, especially children like you. Safety is very important for everyone. Did you know? Many countries have special teams and technologies to keep their people safe. It's a big job for grown-ups!

Where Are Iran and Israel?

Let's remember, Iran and Israel are very, very far from our homes in Africa. They are in a part of the world called the Middle East. Imagine looking at a big map of the whole world. You would see Africa, big and beautiful. Then, you would have to travel quite a distance across the land and sea to find Iran and Israel. This news happened far, far away from your village, town, or city. It doesn't mean that it's happening near you. You are safe in Africa, and this news is just something important that grown-ups around the world are talking about. Did you know? Africa is the second-largest continent in the world! It has 54 amazing countries, all with unique cultures and beautiful people!

What Happens Next?

Now, many grown-ups in countries all over the world are working very hard. They are talking to leaders in Iran and Israel. Their big job is to help everyone calm down and talk about their problems using words, not flying objects. They want peace. They want to make sure that everyone can live safely and without fear. They want countries to be friendly again. It's like when your teacher helps two friends who are arguing to talk and make up. Grown-ups are trying to do that for countries. Did you know? The United Nations is a very big group of countries that work together to solve problems peacefully. They always try to help people talk and be friends!

Our Message of Peace

Remember, it's always best when everyone can live safely, happily, and in peace. We learn to share, be kind, and help each other, right? This is a lesson for countries too! Being kind and understanding helps us all live better lives, no matter where we are in the world. Let's send our wishes for peace to everyone, everywhere, including the people in Iran and Israel. We hope they can find a way to be friends again. Always remember to be kind to your friends, family, and neighbours. Because kindness is the best way to make our world a wonderful place for everyone! Did you know? A simple smile can make a big difference! It can make someone's day brighter and help build a bridge of friendship.
summary: Far away in countries called Iran and Israel, some people in Iran sent big, fast rockets towards Israel because they were unhappy, but many grown-ups are working to make sure everyone is safe and finds a way to be peaceful.
whyMatters: This news is important because it teaches us that sometimes countries have big disagreements, and it reminds us how important it is for everyone, everywhere, to try and talk about problems peacefully and to live safely and kindly with each other.

originalTitle: At least 32 dead after major earthquake strikes southern Philippines
title: A Big Earth Shake in a Far Away Land!
content:
Hi everyone! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you important news from across the world. Even though I'm reporting to you from beautiful Africa, sometimes big things happen far away that we need to know about. Today, we're going to talk about something called an earthquake that happened very, very far from us, in a country called the Philippines. It's a country with lots of islands and blue ocean, just like some places here in Africa!

A Big Shake in the Philippines!

Imagine your toy car shaking really fast, all by itself! That's kind of what happened in a place far away called the Philippines. The ground started to shake very, very strongly. This big shake is called an earthquake. It was a very big earthquake, one of the strongest kinds. Did you know? Our Earth is like a giant puzzle! It's made of many big pieces called 'plates'. These plates are always moving, but very, very slowly. Most of the time, we don't feel them moving. But sometimes, these big pieces of Earth bump into each other or slide past each other. When they do, it makes the ground shake. That shaking is an earthquake! It's like when you try to push two big rocks together, and they suddenly slip and make a rumble.

How Strong Was the Shake?

This earthquake was super strong. Grown-ups use a special number to say how strong an earthquake is. It's called 'magnitude'. This one was a 'magnitude 7.8'. That's a very big number! It means the ground shook with a lot of power. Imagine a little rumble, then imagine a giant drum making a *boom*! This was more like the giant drum making a really big *BOOM!* You could feel the ground moving a lot. Did you know? An earthquake that is 7.0 or higher is considered a 'major' earthquake. That means it can cause lots of shaking and big changes to the land and buildings. The one in the Philippines was even stronger than that! It's a very, very powerful type of shake.

What is a Tsunami?

After the big shake, something else happened. It made the ocean water move in a special way. Sometimes, when there's a very big earthquake under the ocean, it can make giant waves. These giant waves are called 'tsunamis'. Tsunami is a special word that means 'harbour wave' in Japanese. It's like when you drop a stone in a pond and you see ripples, but imagine a super-duper giant stone in the ocean! These waves are not like the small waves you see when you go to the beach to play.

Small Waves, But Still Important

In the Philippines, the earthquake made some small tsunami waves. These waves weren't as big as some really scary tsunamis we sometimes hear about, the ones that can cover whole towns near the sea. But they were still big enough to tell people to be careful near the sea. People living near the beach had to move to higher ground, just in case the waves got bigger. It's always good to be safe and listen to the grown-ups who tell you what to do! Did you know? Tsunami waves can travel across entire oceans! They are not like normal waves you see at the beach that are made by wind. Tsunami waves are made by big movements under the ocean, like earthquakes or volcanoes. They can be very fast, sometimes as fast as an aeroplane, even if they look small in the deep ocean!

Who Else Felt It?

The Philippines is not the only country near this part of the ocean. Other countries also felt the ground shake a little bit or saw those small tsunami waves. Two other countries, Indonesia and Japan, also saw small tsunami waves. They are like neighbours across the water. So, when something big happens in one place, its neighbours often feel it too! Imagine if your friend next door tripped, you might hear the noise or see them fall.

Helping Each Other

When big things like earthquakes happen, people sometimes get hurt, and their homes can get damaged. It's a sad time for many. But people are also very kind. People from all over the world, and even people inside the Philippines, will help those who need it. They will bring food, water, and help fix homes. Helping each other is very important when something difficult happens. It makes people feel loved and safe. Did you know? It's very important to stay calm during an earthquake. If you are inside, you should 'Drop, Cover, and Hold On!' Drop to the ground, crawl under a strong table or desk, and hold onto it until the shaking stops. If you are outside, move to an open area away from buildings and trees. These simple steps can help keep you safe!

What Can We Learn?

Even though this happened far away, we can learn important things. We learn how amazing and powerful our Earth is. It's always moving, even if we don't feel it every day. We also learn how important it is for people to look out for each other and help when others are sad or in trouble. We can send our kind thoughts and wishes to the children and families in the Philippines, Indonesia, and Japan. Remember, we are all part of one big global family! And helping each other is what good friends and neighbours do, no matter how far apart we live. That's all for today's news report!
summary: A very strong earthquake shook the Philippines, causing small ocean waves in nearby countries like Indonesia and Japan, but people are helping each other through this difficult time.
whyMatters: It shows us how powerful our Earth can be when it moves, and how important it is for people all over the world to care for each other and help when sad things happen, even far away.
